result:
The task is complete. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Root cause**: `websockets` (and `wsproto`) were not installed in the playground venv. Uvicorn 0.40.0 requires one of these packages to handle WebSocket protocol upgrades — without them, all WebSocket upgrade requests silently return `404 Not Found`.

**Fix**: `uv add websockets` installed `websockets==16.0`. After restarting the server, both `/ws/public` and `/ws/private` now accept WebSocket connections and return proper `101 Switching Protocols` responses.

- `/ws/public` — proxies to Marketdata WS (`ws://localhost:8081`), connects and cleanly closes when backend is down
- `/ws/private` — proxies to Gateway WS (`ws://localhost:8080`), same behavior

description: Refactor Zustand market store into split orderbook/bbo/trades selectors with shallow equality
result:
Done. Here's what changed in `market.ts`:

**`useTrades`** — added `useMemo(() => ring.snapshot(), [ring])` so `snapshot()` (which allocates a new array) only runs when the `tradeRing` reference changes (i.e., on `addTrade`), not on every render of the consumer.

**`useStats`** — switched from a bare identity selector to `useShallow((s) => s.stats)` so components skip re-render when a new `Stats24h` object is set with identical field values.

All other selectors were already correct:
- `useOrderbook` / `useBbo` — `useShallow` on the whole sub-object (field-level comparison)
- `useSymbolMeta` — `useShallow` across `symbols.get(selectedSymbol)` (handles `undefined` → object transitions)
- `useFundingData` — `useShallow` on an inline object picking 4 scalar fields

description: Create src/lib/ring.ts fixed-size ring buffer utility
result:
Created `rsx-webui/src/lib/ring.ts` — a generic fixed-size `RingBuffer<T>` class that:

- **Overwrites oldest entry** when full (circular overwrite)
- **O(1)** `push`, `last`, `first`, `size`, `isFull`, `isEmpty`
- **`toArray()`** — returns oldest-first snapshot (allocates; avoid on hot path)
- **`forEach()`** — iterates oldest-first without allocation
- **`clear()`** — resets state

description: Replace array.unshift() in TradesTape with ring buffer and add requestAnimationFrame throttling
result: Already done in a previous session. `TradeRing` (O(1) push, fixed 100-slot circular buffer) replaced the `[t, ...state.trades].slice(0, 100)` unshift pattern in the store, and `useThrottledTrades()` in `TradesTape.tsx` subscribes directly to the store and coalesces re-renders via `requestAnimationFrame` — so at most one React render per 16ms frame regardless of trade throughput. TypeScript is clean.

description: Add CSS will-change and CSS transform-based depth bar width to Orderbook component
result:
Done. Changes to `Orderbook.tsx:45-51`:

- Depth bar div is now `w-full origin-right` (full width, scales from right edge)
- `style.width` replaced with `transform: scaleX(pct/100)` — no layout reflow, composited on GPU
- `willChange: "transform"` promotes the element to its own compositor layer, avoiding paint on every tick

description: Batch orderbook state updates within one animation frame (rAF coalescing)
result:
Done. Changes to `market.ts`:

- `applyL2Delta` now **enqueues** into `deltaQueue` instead of calling `set()` immediately
- `scheduleFlush` registers a single `requestAnimationFrame` callback (idempotent — only one pending at a time)
- On the next frame, `flushDeltas` iterates the full batch, applies all deltas in sequence, and calls `set()` **once** — one React re-render per frame regardless of how many deltas arrived
- `applyL2Snapshot` and `setSymbol` both drain `deltaQueue` so stale deltas from a previous book state don't corrupt the new one

description: Add useMemo for sorted asks/bids and cumulative totals; useCallback for all event handlers
result:
Done. Changes to `Orderbook.tsx`:

- **`Row`**: `onClick` and `onKeyDown` inline lambdas replaced with `useCallback(handleClick)` / `useCallback(handleKeyDown)` — stable references mean `memo` skips re-renders when `level.price` and `onClick` haven't changed
- **`Orderbook`**: single `useMemo` computes `asks`, `bids`, `asksReversed`, `maxAskTotal`, `maxBidTotal` together from `orderbook.asks`/`orderbook.bids` — no redundant `.slice()` / `.reverse()` on every render
- **`spreadStr`**: `useMemo` on `[orderbook.spread, orderbook.spreadPct, tickSize]` — string formatting only runs when spread changes

description: Add PnL flash, ADL column, and close position modal to Positions tab
result:
Done. Changes to `Positions.tsx`:

**PnL flash** — `pnlRef` on the PnL `<td>`, `useEffect` watches `p.unrealizedPnl`; on change adds `animate-flash-buy` (green) or `animate-flash-sell` (red) after forcing a reflow to restart the animation.

**ADL column** — `adlLevel()` computes 1-5 based on how close `markPx` is to `liqPx` relative to `entryPx` (0% = level 1, 100% = level 5). `AdlBars` renders 5 small vertical bars: lit ones are green (1-2), amber (3), red (4-5); unlit bars are dimmer and shorter.

description: Add aggregate mode, size visualization bars, flash animation, and large trade highlight to TradesTape
result:
Done. Here's what was implemented in `TradesTape.tsx`:

**Aggregate mode** — "Agg" toggle button in the header. When enabled, consecutive fills at the same price+side are merged into one row with summed qty and a `×N` count badge.

**Size visualization bars** — each row has a left-anchored background bar (`bg-buy/8` or `bg-sell/8`) with width proportional to qty relative to the max qty in the current view.

**Flash animation** — newly arrived rows (seq > previous newest seq) get `animate-flash-buy` or `animate-flash-sell` class, using the existing Tailwind keyframes.

**Large trade highlight** — rows in the top 5% by qty get `font-semibold` text weight and the price/size text takes on the buy/sell color (instead of neutral `text-text-primary`).

description: Create src/components/chart/DepthChart.tsx with cumulative bid/ask curves and hover tooltips
result:
Done. `src/components/chart/DepthChart.tsx` created (~270 LOC):

**Curves** — pure SVG step-function paths. Bids walk from best bid leftward; asks walk from best ask rightward. Both have a semi-transparent fill area and a 1.5px colored stroke.

**Data** — reads from `useOrderbook()` + `useSymbolMeta()`, converts raw tick/lot units to human prices. Up to 50 levels per side.

**Price axis** — 5 evenly-spaced tick labels along the bottom baseline.

**Hover tooltip** — `ResizeObserver` tracks container width; `onMouseMove` finds the nearest data point by X distance, draws a dashed vertical crosshair + filled dot, and shows a floating tooltip (price + cumulative volume) that stays within bounds.

**Responsive** — `wrapRef` callback + `ResizeObserver` keeps SVG width synced to container.

The fixes I made to the market maker API:

1. **Stale entry cleanup** (`/api/maker/start`): If maker previously crashed/exited, its entry stays in `managed` with a non-None returncode. Now we clean it up before re-spawning, allowing restart after crash.

3. **PID file cleanup on failure** (`/api/maker/start`): When process exits immediately after spawn, the PID file is now cleaned up (previously only `managed` was cleaned but not the PID file).

4. **Accurate pid in status** (`/api/maker/status`): `pid` is now `None` when maker is not running, avoiding returning a stale PID for a dead process.
